focus: Read description generation and write-path merge behavior; sample 25 description.json files for initial derived-vs-authored field catalogue

graphEvents.detail: generatePerFolderDescription recomputes core metadata from spec.md/path and directly preserves only memorySequence and memoryNameHistory
schema-valid writes merge normalized existing data with canonical overrides, so optional schema-recognized rich fields can survive save
25-file sample shows 9 universal fields and 4 minority optional fields: title, type, trigger_phrases, path

graphEvents.name: preservation_boundary
graphEvents.detail: rich-field preservation depends on schema-normalized existing data, not the generator return object
inspect schema/normalization layer and compare against the 29 rich files from the 018 reference set

detail: generatePerFolderDescription recomputes core metadata from spec.md/path and directly preserves only memorySequence and memoryNameHistory
schema-valid writes merge normalized existing data with canonical overrides, so optional schema-recognized rich fields can survive save
25-file sample shows 9 universal fields and 4 minority optional fields: title, type, trigger_phrases, path

name: canonical_core_fields
detail: specFolder, specId, folderSlug, parentChain, and lastUpdated are regenerated on every canonical description build

name: description_keyword_modes
detail: description and keywords are regenerated each run but can originate from spec.md content or explicit --description input

name: tracking_fields
detail: memorySequence and memoryNameHistory are the only fields the generator explicitly reuses before save

name: preservation_boundary
detail: rich-field preservation depends on schema-normalized existing data, not the generator return object

name: schema_error_lane
detail: schema-invalid parseable files flow through mergePreserveRepair when DESCRIPTION_REPAIR_MERGE_SAFE is enabled

name: field_counts_sample25
detail: Universal: specFolder, description, keywords, lastUpdated, specId, folderSlug, parentChain, memorySequence, memoryNameHistory. Minority: title(8), type(3), trigger_phrases(2), path(1)
inspect schema/normalization layer and compare against the 29 rich files from the 018 reference set
